key_insight: This paper establishes the first international consensus guidelines for diagnosing and managing Wolfram syndrome using a Delphi process across multiple specialties.
clinical_relevance: The recommendations aim to standardize multidisciplinary care for patients with WFS1- and CISD2-related Wolfram syndrome worldwide, where no licensed disease-modifying treatment currently exists.
method: Drafted consensus statements across 6 clinical domains
Validated through a 3-round modified Delphi process with international experts, patients, families, and advocacy groups
evidence_signal: All 35 final consensus statements met the pre-specified threshold of at least 80% agreement among international reviewers and stakeholders.
watch_item: The guidelines are consensus-based rather than trial-derived, so prospective audit and real-world validation will be important during implementation.
paper_title: Consensus Recommendations for the Clinical Management of Wolfram syndrome Using a Delphi Method
